headline: How SK Hynix Can Save the AI Rally—or Send Tech Stocks Tumbling Again
summary: SK Hynix’s planned U.S. listing this week will test the AI-fueled chip rally, offering investors a chance to buy a memory chip maker not named Micron Technology—or to stay away from a group that may have come too far, too fast.  U.S. investors can get exposure to the country with the iShares MSCI Korea exchange-traded fund, but buying  Samsung Electronics  or SK Hynix individually has been almost impossible.

headline: Apple's Rally: Pricing Power, AI Discipline, And The Memory Crunch
summary: Apple (AAPL) stock dropped 6% on June 25 after the company raised Mac and iPad prices by $100 to $300, blaming a memory shortage CEO Tim Cook called a "hundred-year flood." Less than two weeks later, shares have clawed back most of that loss, trading near 52-week highs. The market appears to be recalculating Apple's position on two fronts: how well placed Apple is inside the DRAM shortage relative to its peers and how its restraint on AI spending looks increasingly like a smart move.

raw.headline: Apple's Rally: Pricing Power, AI Discipline, And The Memory Crunch
raw.summary: Apple (AAPL) stock dropped 6% on June 25 after the company raised Mac and iPad prices by $100 to $300, blaming a memory shortage CEO Tim Cook called a "hundred-year flood." Less than two weeks later, shares have clawed back most of that loss, trading near 52-week highs. The market appears to be recalculating Apple's position on two fronts: how well placed Apple is inside the DRAM shortage relative to its peers and how its restraint on AI spending looks increasingly like a smart move.

Micron shares have fallen 14% in the past five trading sessions, although the stock was up 2.9% to $1,003.33 in Monday’s trading, rising along with the  the  and the  There has been no clear reason for the selloff beyond nervousness around the wider technology sector.  Micron has become a volatile stock, with a nearly 700% gain in the past 12 months.

headline: Are DRAM Stocks Too Hot To Handle Yet or Is This Just the Start of a Bigger Move?

headline: Micron, Ford sign semiconductor supply agreement for vehicles
summary: Micron Technology and Ford Motor on Monday signed a long-term agreement to ‌secure the supply for memory and storage ‌platforms used in the automaker's next-generation vehicle production. This comes days after ​Micron's similar agreement with General Motors, as part of the chipmaker's ongoing investments to expand manufacturing in the United States for automotive customers, including its expansion ‌of advanced DRAM production ⁠in Virginia. DRAM, or dynamic random-access memory, is a critical component for servers ⁠that power cloud computing, databases and AI workloads.

The AI-driven demand has caused a shift in production priorities...

headline: Wall Street Got the Weak Jobs Report It Wanted. Chip Stocks Wrecked the Rally Anyway.
summary: Thursday's jobs report came in far below forecasts, initially easing pressure on the Federal Reserve, before a fresh slide in memory and semiconductor stocks dragged the Nasdaq lower even as the Dow closed at a record. Tesla sold off despite its best delivery quarter in years, healthcare names caught a bid, and SK Hynix lined up what would be the largest US listing ever by a foreign company.

headline: Tokyo puts billions behind Micron's chip plan
summary: Micron Technology (MU) broke ground Saturday on a ¥1.5 trillion, or $9.3 billion, expansion of its memory chip plant in Hiroshima, Japan, according to a Bloomberg report. Japan’s government is absorbing a meaningful share of the bill, unusual even for an industry used to public subsidies. The ...
